old_claim.note: Grinding Stones & Querns provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct grinding-stone lineage remains because the museum record establishes a paired travertine artifact but does not derive it from Upper Paleolithic grinding slabs or querns.

source_shape.source_locator: Artwork Details identify the material, paired artifact form, Egyptian origin, and New Kingdom date without assigning a precursor tool tradition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The catalog supports the existence of a travertine mortar-and-pestle pair, not a developmental line from flat grinding stones.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Morphological similarity between grinding tools is insufficient to create a causal edge without manufacturing, use-wear, or transmission evidence.

ontology_before: A related grinding-tool category was assumed to be a direct enabler of every mortar-and-pestle artifact.
ontology_after: The museum artifact remains use-neutral and carries no unsupported tool-lineage dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mortar_and_pestle no longer directly depends on grinding_stones_querns.
would_reject_if: A source tied this specific Egyptian artifact tradition to a demonstrated manufacturing or use lineage from the scoped Upper Paleolithic grinding stones.
short_reason: The object record does not establish a lineage from grinding slabs or querns.
